name: Sausage and Stilton Quiche
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup pliable but cool butter (the better the quality, the better the crust)
1/4 pound baby Swiss or Stilton cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, salt, and sugar into a bowl.
2. Add egg and mix with ice water until a firm dough forms. Do not knead; shape into a ball, wrap in foil, and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
3. When ready to use, roll the dough on a lightly floured surface (preferably marble) to less than 1/8-inch thick.
4. Lightly coat a quiche pan with butter, then wipe away excess butter with a paper towel.
5. Fit the dough into the pan and trim it so that it is 1/4-inch larger than the edge of the pan. Crimp the edges.
6. Prick the bottom in about eight places with a knife. Line the crust with foil or waxed paper, making sure it reaches up the sides; cut to fit if necessary.
7. Remove from oven and discard foil/waxed paper. Return to oven for 5 minutes to dry.
8. Pour in custard filling: sauté onions in butter until soft, then mix cornstarch, salt, pepper, cream, milk, and eggs together without beating.
9. Place onion mixture on the prebaked crust, sprinkle with cheese, and pour the custard over everything.
10. Bake at 450°F (preheated to lowest shelf) for 12 minutes, remove from oven, return pan to oven for 5 minutes to dry, then bake at 350°F until set like custard (about 1/2 hour to 1 1/2 hours).
